title: Inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for how Kernel and /vote are currently positioned. Focus on CODEX.md, README.md, .codex/prompts/kernel.md, .codex/prompts/vote.md, scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh, scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh, scripts/local/launchers/*. Return the min...
summary_text: [Minimum file set to inspect for `/vote` vs `kernel` positioning](required for this design check): - [CODEX.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/CODEX.md) - [README.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md) - [.codex/prompts/kernel.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md) - [.codex/prompts/vote.md](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/vote.md) - [scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh) - [scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh) - [scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh) - [scripts/local/launchers/kernel](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/kernel) - [scripts/local/launchers/vote](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/vote) - [scripts/local/launchers/v](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers/v) Exact contradictions against a “`/vote`-first, `kernel`-secondary” target: 1. Fresh-session bootstrap is explicitly kernel-first. - [CODEX.md:19] says run `/kernel` before non-trivial work in a fresh Codex session at repo root. - This directly conflicts with a strict vote-first bootstrap policy. 2. Plain shell startup remains kernel-prioritized. - [scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh:196-199] dispatches to `kernel` via `kernel-root` check before plain Codex fallback. - No analogous automatic `/vote`-first branch exists for non-slash startup. - This makes `kernel` the default control-plane entry for that path. 3. Local launcher command path prioritizes kernel flow. - [README.md:319] marks `kernel`/`codex-prompt-launch kernel` as the recommended local execution path. - [scripts/local/launchers/kernel:67-70] attempts latest-open, noninteractive handoff, and guarded launch before fallback to `codex-prompt-launch kernel`; no `/vote` pre-branch exists. - This is kernel-first by command path. 4. Kernel contract checks are written around kernel-first startup, not vote-first. - [scripts/check-codex-kernel-prompt.sh:473-475] smoke checks only `/kernel` and `/k` startup paths. - For a vote-first default design, this would likely need to be reweighted/augmented with `/vote` startup sequencing assertions. What is already aligned with vote-first as secondary: - [CODEX.md:56] already says non-critical work should run under local `/vote` by default, kernel only exceptional. - [CODEX.md:165], [kernel prompt:21], and [vote prompt] together describe `/vote` as local continuation / consensus mode, so `/vote` is semantically positioned as a continuation path, not the main bootstrap path.
